step.title: Load Bundle
step.goal: Read the bundle plus the severity ladder reference. ticket-verify needs the full ticket body in head to walk it section-by-section against the ladder.

substep.title: Locate the Bundle
substep.ask:
Which ticket bundle should I verify?
The bundle should have a fully-drafted ticket body — every section from the Structure Outline present — produced by ticket-write. If sections are missing, run ticket-write to completion first.
substep.action: Validate. Read the Structure Outline from the WORKLOG; compare its section list to the level-2 headings currently in the ticket body (the deliverable). If sections are missing, halt and direct the user back to ticket-write.

substep.title: Read Bundle Files
substep.action: Read the ticket, research, questions, and worklog files completely. Extract: - Primary tag, slug - The drafted ticket body in full - The worklog's Design Summary + Structure Outline (drift checks) - Research findings (used as evidence base for self-review) - Q&A entries (used to detect Q&A→ticket drift) - Linked DRs (read each one; the ticket body should not contradict any DR's decision) - The last_updated_note of the ticket (may contain a strike count from ticket-design or a force-exit flag from ticket-grill — those are findings inputs)

substep.title: Load Severity Ladder
substep.action: Read {{base_dir}}/references/severity-ladder.md into working memory. The ladder defines the four severity levels with examples, the finding output format, and the resolution discipline. Steps 2-4 reference it.

substep.title: Display Load Summary
